mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation confirms robust aggregate demand, which compresses risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals supply-side recovery and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistical significance level above 90% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding corporate revenue capacity and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 12-month periods or positive return frequency falls below 50% during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months where HOUST drops below 1400.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index create stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined upward movements in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL result in positive 20-day forward returns in over 65% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.
